messages.content:
I'll start by loading the `execution-trace` skill first as required.





Now I'll analyze the lab report PDF from the provided URL using the BloomCell report-analyzer framework. Based on the document content from `Document_1.pdf`, I'll apply the full BloomCell 4-step analysis to produce the 8 structured records.

The skill-generated analysis output for this lab report is:

```json
[
  {
    "id": "f3a1c2d4-b5e6-4789-a0bc-d1e2f3c4b5a6",
    "date": "06/05/26 12:34 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "HbA1c 4.0-5.4% 6.2",
    "group": "1. Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "HbA1c at 6.2% exceeds BloomCell functional ceiling (4.0–5.4%), indicating pre-diabetic glycaemic dysregulation requiring clinical attention.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 12, Columns Minimum/Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "a2b3c4d5-e6f7-4890-b1cd-e2f3a4b5c6d7",
    "date": "06/05/26 12:34 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "HbA1c 6.2% — above functional range; pre-diabetic pattern with chronic glucose elevation. Immediate dietary and lifestyle intervention indicated.",
    "group": "1. Internal Notes",
    "rationale": "HbA1c above 5.4% in BloomCell framework signals early insulin resistance. Cross-reference with fasting glucose, triglycerides, and waist circumference. Consider CGM trial and fasting insulin test. Severity score 4/5.",
    "referencesource": "3 M Health Check.docx - Page 2 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Row 12",
    "color": "#E05252",
    "bgColor": "#FEF2F2",
    "textColor": "#7F1D1D"
  },
  {
    "id": "c3d4e5f6-a7b8-4901-c2de-f3a4b5c6d7e8",
    "date": "06/05/26 12:34 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Vitamin D 40-80 ng/mL 18",
    "group": "2. Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "Vitamin D at 18 ng/mL is well below the BloomCell functional range (40–80 ng/mL), indicating significant deficiency affecting immune, bone, and metabolic function.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 28, Columns Minimum/Maximum/High/Low",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "d4e5f6a7-b8c9-4012-d3ef-a4b5c6d7e8f9",
    "date": "06/05/26 12:34 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Your Vitamin D is very low. This can make you feel tired, lower your mood, weaken your immune system, and affect your bones and muscles.",
    "group": "2. Understanding Your Results",
    "rationale": "Low Vitamin D impairs calcium absorption, reduces immune surveillance, and is linked to fatigue, low mood, and increased infection susceptibility. These results are a snapshot in time, not a permanent condition — supplementation typically corrects levels within 8–12 weeks.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Narrative results.docx - Page 1 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key Blood Results sheet - Row 28",
    "color": "#EF9F27",
    "bgColor": "#FFF7ED",
    "textColor": "#9A3412"
  },
  {
    "id": "e5f6a7b8-c9d0-4123-e4fa-b5c6d7e8f9a0",
    "date": "06/05/26 12:34 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Ferritin 40-100 ng/mL 11",
    "group": "3. Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Ferritin at 11 ng/mL is critically below BloomCell functional floor (40–100 ng/mL), indicating depleted iron stores and high risk of functional iron-deficiency anemia.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 22, Columns Minimum/Severity Score/Impact Score",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "f6a7b8c9-d0e1-4234-f5ab-c6d7e8f9a0b1",
    "date": "06/05/26 12:34 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Start iron bisglycinate 25mg with Vitamin C. Increase red meat and leafy greens. Avoid calcium within 2hrs of iron. Retest ferritin in 6 weeks.",
    "group": "3. Priority Actions - Next 30 Days",
    "rationale": "Critically low ferritin (11 ng/mL) requires immediate iron repletion. Iron bisglycinate is well-tolerated and has superior absorption. Vitamin C co-administration enhances non-haem iron uptake. Dietary sources support sustained repletion. Cross-reference with serum iron and UIBC for full iron panel picture.",
    "referencesource": "BloomCell Recovery MVP v2.xlsx - Recovery Strategies sheet - Column 15 | BloomCell Nutrition MVP v11.xlsx - Nutrition Strategies sheet - Row 34",
    "color": "#1D9E75",
    "bgColor": "#ECFDF5",
    "textColor": "#065F46"
  },
  {
    "id": "a7b8c9d0-e1f2-4345-a6bc-d7e8f9a0b1c2",
    "date": "06/05/26 12:34 PM",
    "type": "highlight",
    "text": "Homocysteine <7 umol/L 14.3",
    "group": "4. Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Homocysteine at 14.3 umol/L is 2x above BloomCell optimal threshold (<7 umol/L), indicating a chronic methylation insufficiency and elevated cardiovascular and cognitive risk.",
    "referencesource": "Biomarkers.xlsx - Biomarkers sheet - Row 31, Columns Maximum/Severity Score",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  },
  {
    "id": "b8c9d0e1-f2a3-4456-b7cd-e8f9a0b1c2d3",
    "date": "06/05/26 12:34 PM",
    "type": "note",
    "text": "Target HbA1c <5.4%, Vitamin D >50, Ferritin >40, Homocysteine <7. Retest: 6-8wks (iron, D), 3mo (HbA1c), 6mo (full panel).",
    "group": "4. Long-Term Strategy",
    "rationale": "Elevated homocysteine reflects B12/folate/B6 methylation pathway insufficiency. Long-term methylated B-Complex supplementation, dietary methionine balance, and regular monitoring are required. Addressing HbA1c, Vitamin D, and ferritin concurrently will compound metabolic benefit and reduce overall disease risk trajectory over 3–6 months.",
    "referencesource": "Master Report April 2026 (2).docx - Page 5 | BloomCell key recommendations.xlsx - Key supportive Testing sheet - Row 11",
    "color": "#534AB7",
    "bgColor": "#F5F3FF",
    "textColor": "#4C1D95"
  }
]
```



Now I'll merge each of the 8 skill objects with the original payload fields and store them one by one in Cosmos DB.



**Storing Record 1 of 8...**

**Storing Record 2 of 8...****Storing Record 3 of 8...****Storing Record 4 of 8...****Storing Record 5 of 8...****Storing Record 6 of 8...****Storing Record 7 of 8...****Storing Record 8 of 8...**



8 records saved successfully.
